그의 군대가 주적의 수도인 고비르를 정복함.
그의 아들 무함마두 벨로가 소코토 시를 건설하며 칼리프국의 수도가 됨.
국정 운영에서 은퇴하고 만년을 저술과 가르침에 바침.
소코토에서 사망함.

originalEn.quote_en: Meet your adversary as an equal, or do not meet them at all.
originalEn.meaning_en: This wisdom stems from the famous 1622 incident in Luanda where, offered a floor mat instead of a chair by the Portuguese governor, Nzinga had her servant act as her seat. It teaches that self-respect and the assertion of equality are fundamental to any negotiation or relationship of power.

originalEn.quote_en: A kingdom is not just land, but the will of its people to endure.
originalEn.meaning_en: After being forced from Ndongo, Nzinga did not give up but built a new state in Matamba. This signifies that true strength lies not in territory, but in the resilience and determination of a people to unite and resist, even in the face of adversity.

originalEn.quote_en: To lead is to carry the future of your people, a burden that permits no rest.
originalEn.meaning_en: Nzinga spent nearly four decades in constant warfare and diplomacy to protect her people. This quote emphasizes that true leadership is not about personal glory but about relentless responsibility and sacrifice for the survival and prosperity of one's community.

originalEn.event: 은징가 음반데가 은동고 왕국의 통치 가문에서 태어남.
포르투갈 총독과의 평화 조약 협상을 위해 루안다로 유명한 외교 사절단에 나섬.
오빠 응골라 음반데의 사망 이후 은동고의 여왕이 됨.
은동고에서 쫓겨난 후, 마탐바 왕국을 정복하고 그곳을 자신의 새로운 권력 기반으로 확립함.
최근 포르투갈로부터 루안다를 점령한 네덜란드와 전략적 동맹을 맺음.
포르투갈과 평화 조약을 체결하며 포르투갈은 그녀를 마탐바와 은동고의 통치자로 공식 인정함.
약 80세의 나이로 사망하며 안정되고 주권적인 왕국을 남김.

originalEn.quote_en: A strong army is the arms and legs of an empire, but just law is its heart.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects his belief that military might alone cannot sustain an empire; a fair and unified rule of law is the core of stability and prosperity.

originalEn.quote_en: He who rests on yesterday's victory prepares for tomorrow's defeat.
originalEn.meaning_en: This highlights the importance of constant military innovation and vigilance. He actively embraced new technologies and tactics, such as the introduction of firearms.

originalEn.quote_en: Faith in Allah binds our people together like the sands of the desert.
originalEn.meaning_en: This shows his governing philosophy of using Islam as the foundation for a unified imperial identity and social cohesion.

originalEn.event: 카넴-보르누 제국의 마이(술탄)로 즉위함.
오스만 제국과 외교적 접촉을 수립하여 화기와 군사 고문을 확보하게 됨.
330회 이상의 군사 작전을 수행하며, 특히 서쪽의 하우사족 국가들과 동쪽의 불랄라족에 맞서 제국의 국경을 확보함.
샤리아 법에 기반한 법적 및 행정적 개혁을 시행하고, 벽돌 모스크를 건설하며 이슬람 학문을 장려함.
메카로 하지(순례)를 수행하여 광범위한 이슬람 세계와 외교적, 문화적 유대를 강화함.
군사 작전 중 사망하며 강력하고 중앙집권적이며 번영하는 제국을 남김.

originalEn.quote_en: Actions, not origins, determine a person's true worth.
originalEn.meaning_en: It signifies the attitude of proving one's value through endless effort, rising from a commoner to being revered as a god.

originalEn.quote_en: A thirst for knowledge is the driving force behind creating an immortal legacy.
originalEn.meaning_en: It demonstrates achieving timeless accomplishments by endlessly learning and exploring in various fields such as medicine and architecture.

originalEn.quote_en: Wisdom that reads the times allows one to build seemingly impossible dreams on the bedrock of reality.
originalEn.meaning_en: It refers to the exceptional insight and execution that conceived and realized massive stone structures that never existed before.

originalEn.event: 이집트 제3왕조 조세르 왕의 재상이자 최고 사제로 봉직함.
사카라의 계단 피라미드를 설계하고 건설을 감독하였으며, 이는 세계 최초의 대규모 석조 건축물임.
대략적인 사망 연도. 그의 무덤은 발견되지 않았으나 사카라에 숨겨져 있을 것으로 추정됨.
신왕국 시대에 임호테프에 대한 숭배가 시작되어, 의학과 지혜의 반신으로 존경받음.
후기 왕조 시대, 특히 페르시아 정복 이후, 임호테프는 공식적으로 완전한 신의 지위로 격상되어 자신만의 숭배 의식과 신전을 가짐.

originalEn.quote_en: God is one, without form, parts, or attributes. He is different from all creation.
originalEn.meaning_en: This summarizes Ibn Tumart's core doctrine of 'Tawhid,' emphasizing God's absolute unity and immateriality, rejecting the anthropomorphic interpretations prevalent at the time.

originalEn.quote_en: The Mahdi is infallible, and his command is the command of God.
originalEn.meaning_en: By declaring himself the Mahdi, Ibn Tumart gave his religious and political authority an unquestionable legitimacy, demanding absolute obedience from his followers.

originalEn.quote_en: To command the right and forbid the wrong is the duty of every believer.
originalEn.meaning_en: This principle was the call to action for his reform movement. He believed every follower had an active duty to confront moral and religious corruption in society.

originalEn.event: 모로코 남부 안티아틀라스 산맥의 이길리즈 마을에서 태어남.
코르도바, 바그다드, 다마스쿠스 같은 도시에서 이슬람 신학 및 법률을 공부하기 위해 동쪽으로 여행을 시작함.
마그레브로 돌아와 개혁주의 교리를 설교하기 시작했고, 이로 인해 여러 도시에서 추방당함.
자신이 무오류의 마흐디임을 선포하고 추종자들에게 그렇게 인정받음.
틴멜 산악 요새에 본부를 설립하고 알모하드 운동을 조직함.
집권 알모라비드 왕조에 대항하는 알모하드 전쟁을 이끔.
알모하드족이 첫 주요 승리를 거두기 3년 전 틴멜에서 사망함. 그의 사망은 후계자인 압드 알 무으민에 의해 3년간 비밀에 부쳐짐.

originalEn.quote_en: True power comes not from gender, but from the will to surpass one's own limits.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the indomitable courage of rising as a great ruler by leading countless battlefields as a woman and a warrior.

originalEn.quote_en: A great spear can only show its true power when there is a sturdy shield.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means the strategic foresight to expand territory while protecting the people by building massive walls.

originalEn.quote_en: A leader is not one who directs from behind, but one who charges first into the battlefield.
originalEn.meaning_en: It refers to the leadership of setting an example by spending one's entire life on the fierce battlefield rather than in a safe palace.

originalEn.event: 아미나는 자자우에서 투룬쿠의 바크와 여왕의 딸로 태어남.
마가지야(왕위 계승자)로 지명되고 국정 운영 및 전쟁 훈련을 시작함.
오빠 카라마의 사망 이후 자자우 왕좌에 오름.
34년간의 군사 작전을 수행하며 자자우의 영토와 영향력을 확장함.
자신의 진영과 도시 주변에 '가누와르 아미나'(아미나의 벽)로 알려진 요새화된 흙벽 건설을 시작함.
현재 나이지리아 비다 근처 아타가라에서의 군사 작전 중 사망함.

originalEn.quote_en: O Lord, who created me and planted in me the light of reason.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects Yacob's core belief that reason is a divine gift bestowed upon all humans and is the primary tool for investigating truth.

originalEn.quote_en: All men are equal. Slavery is contrary to the law of God, which is the law of human liberty.
originalEn.meaning_en: Through reason, Yacob concluded that all humans are fundamentally equal, a radical social and ethical claim for his time.

originalEn.quote_en: In matters of faith, a man must follow his own reason and conscience.
originalEn.meaning_en: He criticized religious dogmatism, arguing that an individual's rational judgment and conscience should take precedence over blind faith.

originalEn.event: 에티오피아 제국의 악숨 근처에서 태어남.
수사학, 신학, 시편에 대한 전통적인 에티오피아 교육을 받음.
가톨릭으로 개종하고 에티오피아 정교회를 탄압하던 수세뇨스 황제의 박해를 피해 도주함.
2년간 동굴에서 자발적 유배 생활을 하며 자신의 철학적 사상을 발전시킴.
파실리데스 황제가 수세뇨스의 뒤를 이어 정교회 신앙을 회복시키고 야코브는 은둔에서 나옴.
제자인 왈다 헤이와트의 요청으로 자신의 논문인 '하타타'(탐구)를 저술함.
93세의 나이로 사망함.

originalEn.quote_en: Education is the most powerful tool for liberation from oppression.
originalEn.meaning_en: Chilembwe believed that knowledge and critical thinking were the keys to breaking the mental and material chains of colonial rule.

originalEn.quote_en: If we must shed blood, it should be for our own freedom, not for another's empire.
originalEn.meaning_en: This captures his opposition to the conscription of Africans into World War I, reflecting his belief that African lives should be spent in the cause of African liberation.

originalEn.quote_en: Let not the voice of your own blood be silenced. To be silent is to condone the oppressor.
originalEn.meaning_en: Chilembwe believed it was a moral imperative to speak out against injustice. He taught that inaction was a form of tacit support for the oppressive system.

originalEn.event: 니아살랜드(현 말라위) 치라줄루 지역에서 태어남.
선교사 조셉 부스와 함께 미국으로 건너가 교육을 추구함.
버지니아주 린치버그의 한 교회에서 침례교 목사로 서품됨.
니아살랜드로 돌아와 치라줄루에 프로비던스 산업 선교회(PIM)를 설립함.
제1차 세계 대전 중 니아사 병사들의 징집에 항의하는 열정적인 편지를 지역 신문에 기고함.
1월에 반식민지 봉기를 이끌었으며, 2월 초 식민지 경찰에 의해 살해됨.
니아살랜드가 말라위로 독립함. 존 칠렘브웨는 공식적으로 국가 영웅으로 인정받음.

originalEn.quote_en: I am not yet a king, but the son of a king.
originalEn.meaning_en: This shows an awareness of the responsibility and authority that comes with being the heir apparent, even before ascending the throne, suggesting his innate leadership.

originalEn.quote_en: What have I done to the white people? I have not killed any of them.
originalEn.meaning_en: This highlights his perspective that the war was an act of aggression by the British, not a conflict he sought. He saw his position as defensive.

originalEn.quote_en: How can I be a king? The English will not let me have a single soldier.
originalEn.meaning_en: This expresses his frustration with the hollow nature of his restored kingship, stripped of the military power that defined Zulu leadership.

originalEn.event: 줄루 왕 음판데의 아들로 태어남.
은돈다쿠수카 전투에서 형제 음부야지를 물리치고 상속자로서의 지위를 확보함.
아버지 사망 후 줄루 왕국의 왕으로 즉위함.
영국으로부터 군대 해산을 요구하는 최후통첩을 받고, 이는 영-줄루 전쟁으로 이어짐.
이사들와나 전투에서 영국군에 대한 주요 승리를 거둠.
울룬디 전투에서 패배하고 은신을 강요당함.
영국군에 의해 체포되어 케이프타운으로 유배됨.
런던으로 건너가 빅토리아 여왕을 만나 복위를 간청함.
약화된 권한으로 왕위에 복위되었으나, 이는 내전으로 이어짐.
에쇼웨에서 사망하였으며, 심장마비 또는 독살로 추정됨.

originalEn.quote_en: A monument is not built with stone, but with a unified will.
originalEn.meaning_en: The greatest structures are defined less by their physical materials and more by the collective determination and purpose that brought them into existence.

originalEn.quote_en: To build for eternity, one must command the present with absolute vision.
originalEn.meaning_en: Creating a lasting legacy requires strong leadership that can direct all current resources and manpower toward a firm, clear objective.

originalEn.quote_en: The path to the afterlife is paved not with gold, but with order and divine right.
originalEn.meaning_en: True immortality and divinity come not from the accumulation of wealth, but from upholding Ma'at (cosmic order) and fulfilling one's sacred duties as pharaoh.

originalEn.event: 파라오 스네프루와 헤테프헤레스 1세 여왕의 아들로 태어남.
이집트의 왕위에 올라 제4왕조의 두 번째 파라오가 됨.
자신의 무덤이 될 기자 대피라미드 건설을 지시하고 감독함.
시나이 반도의 와디 마그하라로 탐험대를 파견하여 터키석과 구리 채굴을 후원함.
약 23년간 통치 후 사망하였으며, 기자 대피라미드에 안장되었을 것으로 추정됨.

originalEn.quote_en: True power comes not from the sword, but from the wisdom that moves the mind of the man who wields it.
originalEn.meaning_en: Military might is not enough. Diplomacy, intellect, and persuasion are the most powerful weapons, capable of moving empires.

originalEn.quote_en: Alliances are born of necessity, but they are sustained by passion.
originalEn.meaning_en: Political relationships are built on mutual interest, but they are fragile without personal connection and deep understanding. Winning a heart can be more crucial than winning land.

originalEn.quote_en: History may be written by the victors, but legend is forged by those who refuse to be vanquished.
originalEn.meaning_en: Though my kingdom fell, I chose my own fate rather than submit to my enemy. My final act of defiance ensured I would be remembered not as a mere loser, but as an eternal symbol.

originalEn.event: 이집트 알렉산드리아에서 프톨레마이오스 왕조의 일원으로 태어남.
아버지 사망 후, 어린 동생 프톨레마이오스 13세와 공동 통치자로 즉위함.
동생의 궁정에서 추방된 후, 율리우스 카이사르를 만나 왕위 회복을 위한 도움을 요청함.
카이사르의 아들로 널리 알려진 카이사리온을 출산함.
로마에서 율리우스 카이사르가 암살당하자 클레오파트라는 이집트로 돌아가 자신의 지위를 확보함.
타르수스에서 마르쿠스 안토니우스를 만나 전설적인 정치적, 로맨틱 동맹을 시작함.
악티움 해전에서 클레오파트라와 안토니우스의 군대가 옥타비아누스에게 결정적으로 패배함.
옥타비아누스에게 사로잡히는 것을 피하기 위해 알렉산드리아에서 자살하였으며, 전통적으로는 독사에 물려 사망한 것으로 알려짐.

originalEn.quote_en: The true holy land is not Jerusalem or Rome, but our own capital, Mbanza Kongo.
originalEn.meaning_en: This teaching shifted the center of Christianity from Europe to Africa, specifically to the Kongo, empowering the people with a sense of religious pride and identity.

originalEn.quote_en: The Kongo must be one. The kings must cease their division and come together to restore the kingdom.
originalEn.meaning_en: Kimpa Vita's movement was not merely religious; it had a clear political goal to unify the Kongo, which had been fractured by civil war.

originalEn.quote_en: Reject the white saints of the missionaries and embrace our own black saints.
originalEn.meaning_en: This was a direct challenge to European religious and cultural dominance, asserting that Africans should reinterpret faith within their own cultural framework.

originalEn.event: 콩고 왕국의 키방구 산 근처에서 킴파 비타가 태어남.
심한 병을 앓은 후, 그녀는 파도바의 성 안토니오의 영혼에 빙의되었다고 주장하며 종교 활동을 시작함.
수천 명의 추종자들(안토니아파)을 이끌고 버려진 수도 음반자 콩고를 재점령하고 재건함.
페드로 4세 국왕에게 충성하는 군대에 의해 체포되어 카푸친 선교사들의 영향 아래 시민 의회에서 이단 혐의로 재판을 받음.
킴파 비타는 자신의 어린 아들과 함께 화형에 처해져 처형당함.

originalEn.quote_en: The sea is behind you, the enemy is before you. There is no retreat for us, only victory or martyrdom.
originalEn.meaning_en: To achieve success, one must commit everything with the resolve that there is no turning back. Only total commitment yields great results.

originalEn.quote_en: Opportunity arrives on the winds of chaos. Only the prepared can ride that wind to ascend.
originalEn.meaning_en: Times of instability are perilous but also hold great opportunity. Only those who are always prepared and vigilant can seize that opportunity to succeed.

originalEn.quote_en: A true leader is one who ignites the fire of faith in the hearts of his soldiers, and makes that flame a torch that illuminates the world.
originalEn.meaning_en: The essence of leadership is not simply to command, but to inspire members with a common purpose and belief, empowering them to achieve great things on their own.

originalEn.event: 북아프리카의 베르베르족 부족에서 태어남.
무사 이븐 누사이르에 의해 탕헤르의 총독으로 임명됨.
지브롤터 해협을 건너 이베리아 반도로 군대를 이끌고 진격함.
과달레테 전투에서 서고트족 로데리크 왕을 격파함.
코르도바와 서고트족의 수도 톨레도를 포함한 주요 도시들을 빠르게 정복함.
알-왈리드 1세 칼리프에 의해 무사 이븐 누사이르와 함께 다마스쿠스로 소환됨.
다마스쿠스에서 잊힌 채 사망함.

originalEn.quote_en: I am a woman. I do not like war. But this treaty... this treaty would amputate the legs of Ethiopia.
originalEn.meaning_en: A powerful statement denouncing the deceptive Treaty of Wuchale, showing her willingness to go to war to protect her nation's sovereignty.

originalEn.quote_en: Release him. If I come back safe, I will pardon him. If I die, he will follow me.
originalEn.meaning_en: Said before marching to the Battle of Adwa about a prisoner who tried to assassinate her, this quote showcases her boldness and confidence as a leader.

originalEn.quote_en: The Sun of Ethiopia will never set.
originalEn.meaning_en: A symbolic statement playing on her name 'Taytu' (the Sun), representing the enduring spirit of Ethiopian independence against any foreign aggression.

originalEn.event: 에티오피아 세미엔 주(州)의 귀족 가문에서 태어남.
훗날 메넬리크 2세 황제가 되는 셰와의 사흘레 마리암과 결혼함.
미래 에티오피아의 수도가 될 아디스 아바바('새로운 꽃')의 부지를 선정하고 이름을 지어줌.
메넬리크 2세의 즉위와 함께 에티오피아의 황후가 됨. 그녀는 우찰레 조약의 이탈리아어 버전에 대해 의심을 품음.
아드와 전투에서 자신의 군대를 이끌고 메넬리크 2세에게 조언하며 결정적인 전략적 역할을 수행하여 이탈리아에 대한 에티오피아의 결정적인 승리를 이끌어냄.
메넬리크 2세 황제가 사망함. 타이투는 그녀의 영향력을 두려워하는 궁정 경쟁자들에 의해 권력에서 밀려남.
옛 궁궐에 유폐된 채 엔토토에서 사망함.

originalEn.quote_en: Nigeria is too large and too diverse to be ruled under a rigid unitary constitution.
originalEn.meaning_en: This quote encapsulates Balewa's core belief in federalism. He understood that a flexible, decentralized government structure was essential to accommodate the unique needs of Nigeria's many ethnic groups and regions.

originalEn.quote_en: We believe that in African affairs, the voice of Africa must be heard.
originalEn.meaning_en: This statement shows Balewa's commitment to Pan-Africanism and non-alignment. He argued that African nations should determine their own destiny and speak with a unified voice on global issues, free from external interference.

originalEn.quote_en: I reaffirm today my unshaken faith in the future of our country.
originalEn.meaning_en: Often repeated in his independence day speeches, this phrase reflects Balewa's unwavering optimism and faith in the Nigerian people. Despite the immense challenges he faced, he never lost hope in the nation's potential.

originalEn.event: 나이지리아 북부 바우치에서 태어남.
영국 런던 대학교 교육학 연구소에서 공부하기 위해 영국으로 떠남.
북부 의회와 연방 하원의원으로 선출됨.
나이지리아의 초대 최고 장관이 됨.
10월 1일, 독립 나이지리아의 초대 총리가 됨.
나이지리아가 공화국이 되고, 그는 총리직을 계속 수행함.
1월 15일 군사 쿠데타 중에 암살당함.

originalEn.quote_en: A kingdom's strength is measured not only in its armies, but in the monuments it raises to the gods and for its people.
originalEn.meaning_en: True power is shown through lasting cultural and religious contributions, not just military might.

originalEn.quote_en: Even when the northern lands are lost, the heart of the kingdom beats strong in its ancestral home.
originalEn.meaning_en: The loss of territory doesn't mean the end of a nation if its core identity and people remain strong.

originalEn.quote_en: Amun called me from Nubia to restore order and honor the gods in the Two Lands.
originalEn.meaning_en: A pharaoh's rule is divinely sanctioned, a belief used to legitimize his Kushite origins to the Egyptians.

originalEn.event: 이집트와 쿠시의 파라오로 왕위에 오름.
평화와 번영의 시기로, 카르나크, 카와, 제벨 바르칼에서 광범위한 건축 프로젝트가 진행됨.
아시리아의 에사르하돈 왕이 이집트를 침공하여 멤피스를 점령하고 타하르카를 남쪽으로 도피시킴.
에사르하돈 사망 후 타하르카는 잠시 멤피스를 재점령함.
아시리아의 아슈르바니팔 왕이 대규모 침공을 감행하여 테베 시를 약탈함.
나파타에서 사망하고 누비아의 누리 피라미드에 안장됨.

originalEn.quote_en: The blood of martyrs is the seed of the Church.
originalEn.meaning_en: The sacrifice of Christians who die for their faith inspires others to convert, thus growing the Church.

originalEn.quote_en: What indeed has Athens to do with Jerusalem?
originalEn.meaning_en: A rhetorical question questioning the compatibility of pagan philosophy (Athens) with divine revelation (Jerusalem), emphasizing the supremacy of faith over human reason.

originalEn.quote_en: Truth and the hatred of truth come into the world together.
originalEn.meaning_en: He observed that the presentation of divine truth inevitably provokes hostility from those who oppose it.

originalEn.event: 로마령 아프리카 카르타고에서 태어남.
기독교로 개종함.
기독교 변증학의 주요 저서인 『변증론』(Apologeticus)과 『이교도에게』(Ad Nationes)를 저술함.
엄격한 금욕주의를 선호하며 몬타누스주의 운동에 대한 공감을 보이기 시작함.
삼위일체 교리를 정의하는 핵심 저서인 『프락세아스 반박론』(Adversus Praxean)을 저술함.
주류 교회와 완전히 결별하고 자신만의 교파 지도자가 됨.
카르타고에서 사망함.

originalEn.quote_en: I will not be called a king of provinces but the Emperor of Ethiopia.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects my core ambition to end the fragmentation of the 'Era of the Princes' and establish a single, unified imperial authority over the entire nation.

originalEn.quote_en: I know their tactics... I will let them see what a determined people can do.
originalEn.meaning_en: Said before confronting the British, this quote showcases my defiance and national pride in the face of overwhelming foreign military power, even when the odds were against me.

originalEn.quote_en: Rather than fall into the hands of the enemy, I shall kill myself.
originalEn.meaning_en: This final sentiment demonstrates my ultimate commitment to my own and my nation's honor, choosing death over the humiliation of capture by a foreign power.

originalEn.event: 에티오피아 제국 콰라 지역에서 카사 하일루로 태어남.
'쉬프타'(무법자/반란 지도자)로 명성을 얻으며 강력한 군대와 명망을 쌓음.
결정적인 일련의 전투에서 제메네 메사핀트(왕자들의 시대)의 주요 지역 통치자들을 격파함.
에티오피아 황제로 즉위하며, 테워드로스 2세라는 통치명을 사용함.
행정 중앙집권화, 군대 현대화, 봉건 귀족과 교회의 권력 축소를 위한 야심찬 개혁을 시작함.
영국 빅토리아 여왕에게 군사 동맹을 제안하는 서신을 보냈으나, 회신을 받지 못함.
외교적 무시에 대한 응답으로 영국 영사 찰스 던컨 카메론과 다른 유럽인들을 투옥하여 긴장을 고조시킴.
영국이 인질 구출을 위한 대규모 군사 작전인 아비시니아 원정을 시작함.
마그달라 전투에서 그의 군대가 패배한 후, 빅토리아 여왕이 선물한 권총으로 자살하여 포로가 되는 것을 피함.

originalEn.quote_en: Restoring order amidst chaos is an achievement as great as building something new.
originalEn.meaning_en: It signifies the accomplishment of reuniting a nation stained by religious division and conflict, and restoring its traditions.

originalEn.quote_en: Physical pain cannot be an obstacle blocking the responsibilities of the soul.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the patience of never abandoning the duty to rule the country despite suffering from terrible diseases all one's life.

originalEn.quote_en: History sometimes remembers the shortest-burning flame for the longest time.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means a life that achieved eternity by being discovered later with the most brilliant artifacts, despite a very short reign.

originalEn.event: 아마르나 시에서 투탕카텐으로 태어난 것으로 추정됨.
선대 스멘카레 사망 후, 약 9세의 나이로 이집트 왕위에 오름.
이름을 투탕카멘으로 바꾸고 수도를 테베로 되돌린 후, 전통적인 이집트 신들의 체계를 복원하기 시작함.
약 19세의 나이로 사망하여 왕가의 계곡(KV62)에 급히 마련된 무덤에 안장됨.
그의 거의 온전한 무덤이 카나본 경의 자금 지원을 받은 영국 고고학자 하워드 카터에 의해 발견됨.

originalEn.quote_en: The Golden Stool is not a mere throne. It is the soul of our nation, the spirit of our ancestors, and the future of our children.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes the deep spiritual and cultural significance of the stool, representing the unity and continuity of the Ashanti people.

originalEn.quote_en: A single drop of my people's blood is more precious than any treasure in the kingdom. Sometimes, surrender is the bravest form of resistance.
originalEn.meaning_en: This explains his difficult decision to surrender in 1896 as a strategic choice to preserve his people's lives from a futile war.

originalEn.quote_en: They could chain my body and send me across the sea, but they could never imprison the spirit of the Ashanti.
originalEn.meaning_en: This highlights the enduring nature of his people's identity and resilience, which could not be broken by colonial exile or oppression.

originalEn.event: 크와쿠 두아 3세 왕자로 태어남.
내전 기간 후 아산테헤네로 즉위하며, 프렘페 1세라는 왕좌 이름을 취함.
쿠마시의 파괴를 막기 위해 영국 원정대에 항복함. 시에라리온으로 추방됨.
세이셸 제도로 추가 추방됨. 그의 부재 중에 '황금 의자 전쟁'이 발발함.
민간인 신분으로 골드 코스트(현대 가나)로 돌아가는 것이 허용됨.
영국 식민 정부에 의해 쿠마시헤네(쿠마시 추장) 지위로 복권됨.
쿠마시에서 사망함.

originalEn.quote_en: Amun of Napata has granted me to be ruler of every foreign country.
originalEn.meaning_en: A declaration that his conquest and rule were divinely sanctioned and justified by his patron god, Amun.

originalEn.quote_en: As I live, as Re loves me... what is more vile than... not allowing the great to eat the food of the small?
originalEn.meaning_en: A reflection of his philosophy of mercy during conquest, emphasizing a ruler's duty to uphold justice and protect the weak from the strong.

originalEn.quote_en: A king's action is not hidden from what he has done.
originalEn.meaning_en: This shows his belief that true leadership is transparent and that all actions are judged before the gods and history.

originalEn.event: 아버지 카쉬타로부터 쿠시 왕국 왕위를 계승하여 나파타의 왕이 됨.
사이스의 테프나크트의 커지는 세력에 대항하기 위해 이집트로 군사 작전을 개시함.
헤르모폴리스와 수도 멤피스를 포함한 주요 도시들을 점령하며 이집트 정복을 완료함.
자신의 원정을 기념하기 위해 제벨 바르칼의 아문 신전에서 그의 유명한 '승리 비문'을 세움.
사망하여 누비아 최초의 이집트식 피라미드인 엘-쿠루 왕실 묘지에 안장됨.

originalEn.quote_en: In the fight for justice, there can be no compromise.
originalEn.meaning_en: This speaks to my belief in taking an uncompromising stance when advocating for fundamental human rights and national sovereignty.

originalEn.quote_en: Education is the key to emancipation.
originalEn.meaning_en: I firmly believed that an educated citizenry was the most potent force against oppression and for self-governance.

originalEn.quote_en: Unity is our greatest strength; division our greatest weakness.
originalEn.meaning_en: This was my constant appeal to the diverse peoples of Nigeria to overcome their differences and band together for the common goal of independence.

originalEn.event: 영국령 나이지리아의 라고스에서 출생함.
영국에서 토목 기사 자격을 취득하며 나이지리아인 최초의 토목 기사가 됨.
식민지 정부 공직에서 사임하고 개인 측량사로서 행정부의 강력한 비판자가 됨.
나이지리아 최초의 정당인 나이지리아 국민 민주당(NNDP)을 창당함.
라고스 데일리 뉴스를 창간하여 그의 반식민지 활동의 핵심 도구가 되게 함.
남디 아지키웨와 함께 나이지리아 및 카메룬 국민 협의회(NCNC)를 공동 창립함.
전국 정치 순회 중 81세의 나이로 카노에서 서거함.

originalEn.quote_en: That peace which you have made... will be your ruin.
originalEn.meaning_en: From a letter to a Herero chief, warning that the German 'protection treaties' were a deceptive trap that would ultimately destroy their independence.

originalEn.quote_en: The Lord has established various kingdoms on earth. Therefore I wish to remain the independent chief of my land and people.
originalEn.meaning_en: A rejection of German authority, asserting that his right to rule was divinely and legitimately ordained, championing the sovereignty and self-determination of African leaders.

originalEn.quote_en: Come, let us die fighting.
originalEn.meaning_en: His resolution upon deciding to go to war against Germany, showing his preference for honorable resistance over submission.

originalEn.event: 케이프 식민지 펠라(현 남아프리카 공화국)에서 출생함.
아버지 모세스 위트부이 서거 후 위트부이 나마족의 카프테인(족장)이 됨.
헤레로족 추장 마헤레로에게 독일에 대한 '보호 조약' 체결을 경고하는 유명한 서한을 작성함.
수년간의 저항 끝에 독일과의 보호 조약에 서명하도록 압력을 받음.
조약을 파기하고 독일에 선전포고를 하여 광범위한 헤레로-나마족 전쟁에 참여함.
10월 29일 발그라스 근처에서 전사들을 이끌고 독일에 맞서 싸우다 전사함.
나미비아 달러 지폐 모든 권종에 그의 초상이 사용되어 국가 영웅으로서의 지위를 확고히 함.

originalEn.quote_en: The education of women is not a luxury, but a necessity for the progress of a nation.
originalEn.meaning_en: Sha'arawi believed that educated women would become better mothers and citizens, contributing to the advancement of the entire nation.

originalEn.quote_en: The liberation of the homeland cannot be separated from the liberation of its women.
originalEn.meaning_en: She argued that true national independence could only be achieved when the rights and freedoms of half of its population were secured.

originalEn.quote_en: Rights are not given, they are taken.
originalEn.meaning_en: Sha'arawi emphasized that women must demand their own rights through public action and organized resistance, rather than passively waiting for change.

originalEn.event: 이집트 민야에서 명망 있는 부유한 가정에 태어남.
13세에 사촌인 알리 파샤 샤아라위와 강제 결혼함.
이집트 혁명 중 영국 통치에 반대하는 최초의 여성 가두 시위 중 하나를 조직하고 이끌었음.
카이로의 주요 기차역에서 공개적으로 얼굴 베일을 벗어 이집트 페미니즘의 전환점이 됨.
이집트 페미니스트 연합(EFU)을 창립하고 초대 회장이 됨.
EFU가 영향력 있는 페미니스트 잡지 '레지프시엔'(이집트 여성)을 발행하기 시작함.
카이로에서 제1차 아랍 페미니스트 회의를 소집하고 아랍 페미니스트 연합의 창립 회장이 됨.
68세의 나이로 카이로에서 별세함.

originalEn.quote_en: Thou hast made us for thyself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it rests in thee.
originalEn.meaning_en: This expresses the theological view that humanity's deepest longings and anxieties can only find ultimate satisfaction and peace in God, the creator.

originalEn.quote_en: Faith is to believe what we do not see; the reward of this faith is to see what we believe.
originalEn.meaning_en: This means that faith is an act of trust that goes beyond rational proof, and through such devotion, one eventually comes to realize and experience spiritual truth.

originalEn.quote_en: Since you cannot do good to all, you are to pay special attention to those who, by the accidents of time, or place, or circumstances, are brought into closer connection with you.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes the balance between the ideal of universal love and practical application. It states the importance of practicing concrete love for our immediate neighbors within our limited capabilities.

originalEn.event: 누미디아 타가스테(현 알제리 수크 아라스)에서 출생함.
카르타고에서 수사학을 공부하기 시작했으며, 그곳에서 첩을 얻어 아들 아데오다투스를 낳음.
이원론적 종교인 마니교의 신봉자가 됨.
밀라노로 이동하여 수사학 교수로 재직하며 암브로시우스 주교를 만남.
밀라노의 한 정원에서 유명한 기독교로의 회심을 경험함.
밀라노에서 아들 아데오다투스와 함께 암브로시우스에게 세례를 받음.
히포 레기우스(현 알제리 안나바)에서 사제로 서품됨.
히포의 주교가 되어 서거할 때까지 그 직위를 유지함.
자신의 영적 자서전인 '고백록'을 저술함.
이교도들이 로마 함락(410년)이 로마 신들을 버린 탓이라고 주장한 것에 대한 응답으로 '하나님의 도성'을 저술함.
도시가 반달족의 포위 공격을 받는 동안 히포 레기우스에서 서거함.

originalEn.quote_en: Blind imitation only solidifies one's own chains, and true liberation begins with self-discovery.
originalEn.meaning_en: It provides the awakening that one must realize their own unique tradition and identity rather than unconditionally following other cultures.

originalEn.quote_en: The pen and the law code shake the foundations of an unjust system from a deeper place than the sword.
originalEn.meaning_en: It signifies an intellectual struggle that fought against the contradictions of colonial rule through writing and legal resistance instead of violence.

originalEn.quote_en: Divided voices scatter in the wind, but a united cry becomes the thunder that awakens an era.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the value of solidarity in gathering intellectuals as one to achieve rights with a unified voice.

originalEn.event: 골드 코스트 아노마부(현 가나)에서 출생함.
고등 교육을 위해 시에라리온 프리타운의 푸라베이 대학으로 감.
법학을 공부하기 위해 런던 이너 템플로 감.
변호사 자격을 얻고 법률 실무를 위해 골드 코스트로 돌아옴.
영향력 있는 소설 '속박되지 않은 에티오피아: 인종 해방 연구'를 출판함.
해안 지역구의 입법회 의원으로 선출됨.
아크라에서 영국 서아프리카 국민회의(NCBWA)를 창립하고 이끌었음.
골드 코스트 아크라에서 서거함.

originalEn.quote_en: More terrifying than physical chains is the invisible shackle placed on one's own mind.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means that one must face and overcome the self-denial and internal submission created by an oppressive system.

originalEn.quote_en: Spectating knowledge is nothing but a helpless excuse, and true intellect is perfected in acting conscience.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows a practical life that abandoned a comfortable position and jumped into the scene of suffering with the oppressed people.

originalEn.quote_en: To fully heal a wounded soul, the order of a sick world must first be cured.
originalEn.meaning_en: It refers to the insight that pierced through to the fact that the fundamental cause of sickening an individual's mind lies in social contradiction and violence.

originalEn.event: 프랑스 식민지 마르티니크의 포르드프랑스에서 출생함.
제2차 세계대전 중 자유 프랑스군에 합류하기 위해 도미니카로 감.
프랑스 리옹에서 의학과 정신의학을 공부한 후 정신과 의사 자격을 취득함.
첫 저서 '검은 피부, 하얀 가면'을 출판함.
알제리 블리다-조인빌 정신병원 정신과 과장이 됨.
프랑스 식민지 정책에 항의하여 병원 직책을 사임하고 알제리 민족 해방 전선(FLN)에 합류함.
'알제리 혁명 5년: 식민주의는 죽어가고 있다'를 출판함.
장 폴 사르트르의 서문과 함께 그의 가장 유명한 저서 '대지의 저주받은 자들'을 출판함.
36세의 나이로 미국 메릴랜드 베데스다에서 백혈병으로 서거함.

originalEn.quote_en: The problem of the twentieth century is the problem of the color-line.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes that racial discrimination is the most fundamental and significant issue of the 20th-century society, affecting all aspects of politics, economics, and social life.

originalEn.quote_en: It is a peculiar sensation, this double-consciousness, this sense of always looking at one’s self through the eyes of others, of measuring one’s soul by the tape of a world that looks on in amused contempt and pity.
originalEn.meaning_en: This describes the internal division experienced by Black Americans. They experience a psychological state of conflict between the gaze of a white-centric society and their own identity, as both an American and a Black person.

originalEn.quote_en: The Talented Tenth of the Negro race must be made leaders of thought and missionaries of culture among their people.
originalEn.meaning_en: This argues that an elite group of educated intellectuals within the Black community should exercise leadership to advance the progress and rights of the entire race.

originalEn.event: 매사추세츠주 그레이트 배링턴에서 출생함.
하버드 대학교에서 박사 학위를 취득한 최초의 아프리카계 미국인이 됨.
그의 기념비적인 저서 '흑인의 영혼'을 출판함.
흑인의 시민권을 요구하는 나이아가라 운동 창립에 기여함.
전미 유색인종 발전 협회(NAACP)를 공동 창립함.
NAACP 잡지 '더 크라이시스'의 편집자로 활동함.
파리에서 제1회 범아프리카 회의를 조직함.
콰메 은크루마 대통령의 초청으로 가나로 이주함.
95세의 나이로 가나 아크라에서 서거함.

originalEn.quote_en: Aristotle is great, but Plato is greater.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects his belief that Plato dealt with divine, eternal truths, whereas Aristotle focused on more mundane, transient matters.

originalEn.quote_en: We ourselves are the masters of our own souls, and by our own choice we can be good and happy.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes his humanistic belief in human agency and the power of reason to achieve virtue and happiness, rather than relying solely on divine grace.

originalEn.quote_en: The world is eternal, having neither beginning nor end.
originalEn.meaning_en: This is a core tenet that directly challenges the Christian doctrine of creation, reflecting his Neoplatonic and pagan-influenced worldview.

originalEn.event: 비잔틴 제국의 수도 콘스탄티노폴리스에서 출생함.
모레아 전제국의 수도 미스트라스에서 고위 판사이자 고문으로 임명됨.
동방교회와 서방교회의 통합을 목표로 한 비잔틴 대표단의 일원으로 피렌체-페라라 공의회에 참석함.
피렌체에서 아리스토텔레스에 대한 플라톤의 우월성에 대해 영향력 있는 강연을 하여 이탈리아 인문주의자들을 매료시킴.
미스트라스로 돌아와 신플라톤주의에 기반한 새로운 국가를 제안하는 그의 주요하고 논란이 많은 저서 '노모이'(법률)를 저술함.
오스만 제국에 의한 콘스탄티노폴리스 함락 불과 1년 전 미스트라스에서 서거함.
그의 논란이 많은 저서 '노모이'는 전 제자였던 겐나디우스 스콜라리우스 총대주교의 명령으로 공개적으로 불태워짐.

originalEn.quote_en: However stupid a fool's words may be, they are sometimes enough to confound an intelligent man.
originalEn.meaning_en: This shows that the irrationality and chaos of society can disrupt even the most rational minds, highlighting the unpredictable nature of humanity and social absurdity.

originalEn.quote_en: The longer and more carefully we look at a funny story, the sadder it becomes.
originalEn.meaning_en: My humor was not mere entertainment; it was a mask for deep-seated tragedy and social decay, implying the tears hidden within laughter.

originalEn.quote_en: There is nothing more to be feared by a man than laughter.
originalEn.meaning_en: Laughter, especially satirical laughter, is a powerful weapon that dismantles authority and hypocrisy. It can shake the foundations of the corrupt without violence.

originalEn.event: 러시아 제국 폴타바 현 벨리키 소로친치(현 우크라이나)에서 출생함.
'디칸카 근교 농장의 저녁'을 출판하여 첫 주요 문학적 성공을 거둠.
그의 풍자극 '검찰관'을 초연하여 큰 논란을 일으킴.
주로 로마에서 해외 생활을 하며 '죽은 혼'을 집필함.
그의 대표작 '죽은 혼' 1부와 영향력 있는 단편 소설 '외투'를 출판함.
종교적 광기 발작 중에 '죽은 혼' 2부 원고를 불태우고, 열흘 후 심각한 고행으로 서거함.

originalEn.quote_en: Opportunity is not something one waits for, but something one creates.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects his belief in proactive action to generate opportunities, rather than passively waiting for them.

originalEn.quote_en: He who controls the Amur, controls Siberia.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes the strategic and economic importance of the Amur River for control over all of Siberia and the Far East.

originalEn.quote_en: Siberia cannot be seen from St. Petersburg.
originalEn.meaning_en: This expresses his frustration that bureaucrats in the capital could not understand the complex and urgent realities on the ground, justifying his independent actions.

originalEn.event: 러시아 제국 상트페테르부르크에서 출생함.
동시베리아 총독으로 임명됨.
크림 전쟁 중 아무르강을 따라 무단 탐험을 이끌고 러시아 정착지를 건설함.
청나라와 아이훈 조약을 체결하여 아무르강 좌안을 러시아 영토로 확보하고 '아무르스키'라는 칭호를 얻음.
베이징 조약이 러시아의 영토 획득을 확인하고 확장하여 연해주가 될 영토를 할양받음.
중앙 정부와의 의견 불일치로 총독 직에서 사임함.
프랑스 파리에서 서거함.

originalEn.quote_en: The greatest seed is not the one that disappears buried in the ground, but the one that preserves itself for future life.
originalEn.meaning_en: It signifies the pioneering foresight of collecting genetic resources from around the world to save humanity from future crises.

originalEn.quote_en: False power may temporarily imprison the truth, but it cannot kill the life that the truth will bloom.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the fact that despite being sacrificed to political ideology, the academic legacy he left behind saved countless lives.

originalEn.quote_en: The attitude of not abandoning one's beliefs even in the face of starvation is the most solid barrier that protects the world.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means the noble spirit that silently walked its own path without bending the conscience toward truth even at the threshold of death.

originalEn.event: 러시아 제국 모스크바에서 출생함.
페트로프스카야 농업 아카데미(현 모스크바 티미랴제프 농업 아카데미)를 졸업함.
진화 생물학의 핵심 원리인 동종 변이 계열 법칙을 정립함.
레닌그라드 응용 식물국을 이끌었고, 이를 전연방 식물 산업 연구소로 재편함.
그의 획기적인 저서 '재배 식물의 기원 중심지'를 출판함.
우크라이나 탐사 중 NKVD에 체포되어 트로핌 리센코가 주도한 정치적 박해의 희생자가 됨.
사보타주, 간첩 행위 및 기타 날조된 혐의로 사형 선고를 받음. 형은 나중에 20년 징역으로 감형됨.
사라토프 감옥에서 굶주림으로 서거함.
니키타 흐루쇼프의 탈스탈린화 기간 동안 사후에 복권됨.

originalEn.quote_en: There are still so many places in the world to explore. It is only courage that is lacking.
originalEn.meaning_en: Emphasizes the importance of courage to overcome the fear of the unknown and to take on new challenges.

originalEn.quote_en: The greatest reward for an explorer is not gold or fame, but filling the blank spaces on a map.
originalEn.meaning_en: The true value of exploration lies not in material rewards, but in the expansion of knowledge and discovery itself.

originalEn.quote_en: Nature is the greatest teacher. It teaches the deepest wisdom through the harshest trials.
originalEn.meaning_en: Signifies that the hardships and adversities faced in harsh natural environments can become the greatest opportunities for learning and growth.

originalEn.event: 러시아 제국 스몰렌스크 현 킴보로보에서 출생함.
러시아 극동 우수리 지역으로 첫 주요 탐험을 시작함.
첫 중앙아시아 탐험을 수행하며 몽골, 고비 사막을 탐험하고 양쯔강 상류에 도달함.
두 번째 중앙아시아 탐험을 이끌며 롭노르 지역과 준가르 분지를 탐험함.
세 번째 탐험 중 티베트 일부 지역을 탐험하고 프르제발스키 야생마를 최초로 과학적으로 기술함.
네 번째 탐험을 수행하며 고비 사막에서 톈산 산맥을 가로질러 여행함.
다섯 번째 중앙아시아 탐험 계획 시작 시점에 키르기스스탄 카라콜에서 장티푸스로 서거함.

originalEn.quote_en: A true monarch does not fear the disgrace of kneeling, but only waits for the time to rise again in the future.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means the wisdom of ensuring the survival of the people and planning for the future while enduring the humiliation of submitting to a powerful enemy state.

originalEn.quote_en: In a fierce storm, one must know how to read the grain of the wind and adjust the sails rather than fighting it blindly.
originalEn.meaning_en: It refers to the insight of flexibly conducting diplomacy by utilizing the interests of surrounding major powers in a chaotic political situation.

originalEn.quote_en: The first step in recovering a lost legacy begins with protecting the smallest spark of hope.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the perseverance of finally regaining his rights and rebuilding the kingdom despite losing everything and being driven out in his childhood.

originalEn.event: Danylo Romanovych is born.
His father, Roman the Great, is killed in battle. Danylo and his family are forced into exile.
He reclaims the throne of the Principality of Volhynia, establishing his first power base.
He conquers the Principality of Halych, finally uniting Halych and Volhynia under his rule.
His forces capture Kyiv, the ancient capital of Rus', shortly before it is devastated by the Mongol invasion.
He travels to Sarai, the capital of the Golden Horde, to pledge allegiance to Batu Khan to save his kingdom from destruction.
He is crowned 'King of Ruthenia' by a papal legate in Drohiczyn, seeking an alliance with Western Europe.
Danylo Halytskyi dies, leaving behind a powerful, unified kingdom to his successors.

originalEn.quote_en: Without the will to be independent, no external help can rebuild a collapsed house.
originalEn.meaning_en: It signifies the courage of proudly declaring resistance and independently reforming the country even in the midst of an absolute crisis.

originalEn.quote_en: More important than the number of an army is the unwavering mind of soldiers armed with conviction and firmly united.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the determination that led to a miraculous victory with desperate resolve and tactics despite an overwhelming numerical disadvantage.

originalEn.quote_en: A true winner does not stop at conquering territory but blooms the flowers of tolerance and culture on the ruined land.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means the governance that achieved the golden age of the country by encouraging learning and promoting harmony even after defeating the enemy with force.

originalEn.event: 16세의 나이로 조지아의 왕위에 오름.
셀주크 제국에 대한 조공을 중단하고 조지아의 독립을 선언함.
루이시-우르브니시 시노드를 소집하여 조지아 정교회를 개혁함.
군대 강화를 위해 약 4만 가구의 킵차크족을 조지아에 정착시킴.
디드고리 전투에서 대규모 셀주크 연합군을 상대로 결정적인 승리를 거둠.
400년 만에 트빌리시를 해방시키고 조지아의 수도로 삼음.
사망 후, 그의 유언에 따라 겔라티 수도원 정문 아래에 묻힘.

originalEn.quote_en: I have come from a distant land by the command of the King to say this: 'If you will go out to war with us, we will give you supplies and provisions...'
originalEn.meaning_en: This highlights the practical, political nature of his mission, a stark contrast to his spiritual origins.

originalEn.quote_en: Know ye, O our glorious kings, that many of our Fathers have gone into the countries of the Indians, and Chinese, and have preached...
originalEn.meaning_en: This shows his pride in the long history of Eastern Christianity and his role as a representative of a faith unfamiliar to Europeans.

originalEn.quote_en: The king's heart is in the hand of God.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects his attempt to reconcile his spiritual beliefs with the political realities he faced, seeing a divine hand in the actions of powerful rulers.

originalEn.event: Born in Khanbaliq (modern Beijing), Yuan China.
Begins his pilgrimage from China to Jerusalem with his student Rabban Markos.
Rabban Markos is elected Catholicos of the Church of the East as Mar Yaballaha III.
Sent by Ilkhan Arghun as an ambassador to Europe.
Travels through Europe, meeting with Byzantine Emperor Andronikos II, cardinals in Rome, King Philip IV of France, and King Edward I of England.
Returns to the Ilkhanate with letters and gifts from European leaders.
Dies in Baghdad.

originalEn.quote_en: No! I am alive! I will live forever! I have in my heart that which will not die.
originalEn.meaning_en: An expression of indomitable will, stating that even in the face of physical pain and the threat of death, art and spirit survive eternally.

originalEn.quote_en: To hope without hoping.
originalEn.meaning_en: This paradoxical and strong state of mind signifies not giving up hope even in desperate situations. It was a philosophy that permeated her entire life.

originalEn.quote_en: He who is not able to sacrifice himself for his own freedom is not worthy of it.
originalEn.meaning_en: Emphasizes that true freedom is not passively given but is a value that must be won through active struggle and sacrifice.

originalEn.event: Born Larysa Petrivna Kosach in Novohrad-Volynskyi, Russian Empire (now Ukraine).
Develops symptoms of bone tuberculosis, an illness that would affect her for the rest of her life.
Publishes her first poem, 'Lily of the Valley,' in the Lviv journal Zorya at age 13, using the pseudonym 'Ukrainka'.
Her first poetry collection, 'On the Wings of Songs,' is published in Lviv.
Translates the Communist Manifesto into Ukrainian.
Marries folklorist and musicologist Klyment Kvitka.
Writes her most famous work, the fantasy play 'The Forest Song' (Lisova Pisnya), in just twelve days during a period of remission.
Dies in Surami, Georgia, at the age of 42 from her chronic illness.

originalEn.quote_en: Look at the world and its tidings, it is a legend and a dream.
originalEn.meaning_en: This line reflects on the transient nature of worldly events and achievements, suggesting they are as ephemeral as a legend or a dream. It's a call to recognize the impermanence of life and not to become too attached to material possessions.

originalEn.quote_en: The scent of the Mūliyān stream comes to me, the memory of my beloved, kind and dear.
originalEn.meaning_en: From a famous ode written to persuade the homesick king to return to Bukhara, this line showcases Rudaki's power to evoke powerful emotions of nostalgia and affection through sensory details. It demonstrates the emotional power of poetry.

originalEn.quote_en: Any sorrow, when it passes, will be a joy.
originalEn.meaning_en: This expresses the optimistic view that hardship and sorrow are not permanent. In time, even the memory of past suffering can enhance the appreciation of present joy, reflecting a wisdom that finds hope even in adversity.

originalEn.event: Born in Panjrud, a village in Transoxiana, then part of the Samanid Empire (modern-day Tajikistan).
Rises to prominence and becomes the esteemed court poet for the Samanid ruler Nasr II in Bukhara.
Nasr II's patronage of Isma'ili missionaries leads to a Sunni clerical backlash and a court coup.
Following the coup, Rudaki is dismissed from the court, blinded, and returns to his hometown in disgrace.
Dies in poverty and obscurity in his native Panjrud.
His tomb is rediscovered by Soviet and Tajik archaeologists, confirming the biographical accounts of his blinding and final resting place.

originalEn.quote_en: The Turkic and Arabic languages are like two horses running neck and neck.
originalEn.meaning_en: This expresses pride in the Turkic language, asserting that it possesses equal value and richness to Arabic, the central language of the Islamic world at the time.

originalEn.quote_en: An adze does not carve its own handle.
originalEn.meaning_en: A Turkic proverb I recorded. It emphasizes the importance of community and interdependence, suggesting that no person or thing can create or improve itself without external help.

originalEn.quote_en: The adornment of a man is his tongue (language).
originalEn.meaning_en: This means that eloquence and the proper use of language are the highest virtues and defining characteristics of a person's worth and refinement.

originalEn.event: Born in or near Kashgar into the Kara-Khanid Khanate's ruling dynasty.
Flees his homeland due to political strife after his father's failed attempt to seize power.
Travels extensively throughout the lands of the Turkic peoples, collecting linguistic and ethnographic data.
Settles in Baghdad and begins compiling the Dīwān Lughāt al-Turk.
Completes the final version of the Dīwān, presenting it to the Abbasid Caliph.
Dies in Upal, a small city near Kashgar, where his tomb is now a revered site.

originalEn.quote_en: History is not merely a list of past events, but the key to understanding human nature and the essence of power.
originalEn.meaning_en: Through his 'Chronographia,' Psellos delved into the psychology and motives behind events, believing that history provides insight into the present and future.

originalEn.quote_en: Philosophy is like a lamp in the darkness, leading us from ignorance to see true reality.
originalEn.meaning_en: As a devotee of Platonic philosophy, he believed that rational inquiry and philosophical thought could complement faith and lead humans to a higher truth.

originalEn.quote_en: Life in the court is like dancing on a serpent; a moment's carelessness leads to ruin.
originalEn.meaning_en: This saying, born from his own experience, vividly describes the unpredictable and perilous political environment of the Byzantine court.

originalEn.event: Born Constantine Psellos in Constantinople.
Begins his political career at the court of Emperor Michael V.
Appointed 'Consul of the Philosophers' (Hypatos ton Philosophon), effectively head of the University of Constantinople.
Under pressure, he enters a monastery and takes the monastic name Michael.
Recalled to the imperial court by Empress Theodora.
Composes the 'Chronographia,' his most famous historical work.
Believed to have died around this year, though the exact date is uncertain.

originalEn.quote_en: The might of Russia will grow with Siberia.
originalEn.meaning_en: This statement reflects my belief that the vast, untapped resources and potential of Russia's eastern territories, particularly Siberia, were key to the nation's future prosperity and power.

originalEn.quote_en: Nature's laws are simple, but its works are great.
originalEn.meaning_en: This summarizes my view that the fundamental principles governing the universe are elegant and straightforward, yet these simple laws combine to create the astonishing complexity and grandeur of the natural world we observe.

originalEn.quote_en: The amount lost from one body is added to another.
originalEn.meaning_en: This is my early formulation of the law of conservation of mass. It is the cornerstone concept of modern chemistry that matter cannot be created or destroyed, only changed in form.

originalEn.event: Born in the village of Mishaninskaya (later renamed Lomonosovo) in Arkhangelsk Governorate, Russia.
At age 19, leaves his village and walks to Moscow to enroll in the Slavic Greek Latin Academy.
Is sent to Germany to study chemistry, physics, and metallurgy at the University of Marburg.
Returns to Russia and is appointed as an adjunct professor at the St. Petersburg Academy of Sciences.
Establishes the first research and teaching chemical laboratory in Russia.
Co-founds Moscow State University with his patron, Ivan Shuvalov, based on his proposed plan.
Observes the transit of Venus and is the first to discover and document the presence of an atmosphere on the planet.
Dies of pneumonia in St. Petersburg and is buried in the Lazarevskoe Cemetery.

originalEn.quote_en: A clean cause requires clean hands.
originalEn.meaning_en: Emphasizes that political struggle must be conducted with moral integrity; the ethics of the means are as important as the end goal.

originalEn.quote_en: Every man for himself, and God for all, say the English. I would change this proverb and say: Every man for all, and God for every one.
originalEn.meaning_en: A call for collective responsibility and solidarity over individualism, reflecting his socialist and communitarian ideals.

originalEn.quote_en: The development of nationality is a necessary condition for human progress.
originalEn.meaning_en: He saw the healthy development of distinct national cultures and identities not as a source of conflict, but as an essential part of the broader progress of humanity.

originalEn.event: Born in Hadiach, Poltava Governorate, Russian Empire.
Studied at Kyiv University and became a prominent member of the 'Hromada' student society.
Became a docent (lecturer) in ancient history at Kyiv University.
Dismissed from his post following the Ems Ukaz, which banned the Ukrainian language, and went into political exile.
Founded the influential Ukrainian-language political journal 'Hromada' in Geneva, Switzerland.
Accepted a professorship at the University of Sofia in Bulgaria.
Died in Sofia, Bulgaria, and was buried there.

originalEn.quote_en: A nation without its own history is like manure, upon which other nations grow.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes that a nation's identity and future depend on understanding and owning its own history. To lose one's history is to lose one's sovereignty.

originalEn.quote_en: We want an independent Ukraine... not to oppress other peoples, but to govern ourselves on our own land.
originalEn.meaning_en: This clarifies that the goal of Ukrainian independence is not imperial ambition, but the legitimate right to self-determination and self-governance.

originalEn.quote_en: The worst misfortune is not slavery itself, but when the slave is content with his shackles and even admires them.
originalEn.meaning_en: This warns that more dangerous than external oppression is the internal state of mental servitude, where one accepts oppression and loses the will to be free.

originalEn.event: Born in Chełm, Congress Poland, Russian Empire.
Appointed Professor of History at Lviv University in Austria-Hungary.
Publishes the first volume of his magnum opus, 'History of Ukraine-Rus''.
Elected head of the Central Rada (Council) in Kyiv following the February Revolution.
Elected President of the Ukrainian People's Republic.
Lives in exile in Vienna and Prague after the collapse of the republic.
Returns to Soviet Ukraine to continue his academic work.
Arrested by the Soviet secret police (OGPU) and exiled to Moscow.
Dies under mysterious circumstances in Kislovodsk, Russia.

originalEn.quote_en: Though I am not equal to the task of holding it, I have in my own mind resolved to put forth my strength to the utmost.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes a resolve to give one's all and not give up, even when facing overwhelming odds.

originalEn.quote_en: Write in a simple, clear, and plain style; let the meaning of what you write be seen at a glance.
originalEn.meaning_en: This values clarity and directness in communication, reflecting his own style in the Baburnama.

originalEn.quote_en: From my birth, I have had no rest; I have not had a moment's peace.
originalEn.meaning_en: A candid reflection on his life of constant struggle, which also highlights his resilience in enduring it all to found an empire.

originalEn.event: 페르가나 계곡의 안디잔에서 출생.
12세의 나이로 페르가나의 왕위에 오름.
처음으로 사마르칸트를 점령했으나 곧 잃음.
카불을 점령하고 새로운 거점으로 삼음.
제1차 파니파트 전투에서 이브라힘 로디를 격파하고 델리를 점령.
칸와 전투에서 라지푸트 연합군을 격파하고 지배권을 확고히 함.
무굴 제국의 아그라에서 사망.

originalEn.quote_en: An emperor's place is not in the palace, but at the head of his army.
originalEn.meaning_en: This is my belief that true leadership must be exercised not in comfort, but on the ground where the greatest challenges and dangers lie.

originalEn.quote_en: The wealth of the state exists not for luxury, but to sharpen its swords.
originalEn.meaning_en: I shunned personal luxury and focused all of the empire's resources on strengthening its military. This was the only way to protect the realm.

originalEn.quote_en: Mercy is for times of peace; in times of war, decisiveness is mercy.
originalEn.meaning_en: The surest way to end a war quickly is to strike terror into the heart of the enemy. My ruthlessness was a means to prevent greater bloodshed.

originalEn.event: Born as a 'porphyrogennitos' (born in the purple), a son of Emperor Romanos II and Empress Theophano.
Becomes senior emperor after the death of John I Tzimiskes, but real power is held by the eunuch Basil Lekapenos.
Faces and eventually crushes a series of large-scale internal rebellions led by the powerful generals Bardas Skleros and Bardas Phokas.
Forms a crucial alliance with Vladimir I of Kiev, receiving 6,000 Norse mercenaries (the foundation of the Varangian Guard) in exchange for his sister Anna's hand in marriage.
Achieves a decisive victory over the Bulgarian army at the Battle of Kleidion.
Completes the conquest of the First Bulgarian Empire, annexing it fully into the Byzantine Empire.
Dies while planning a military expedition to reclaim Sicily, leaving the empire at its territorial and financial zenith.

originalEn.quote_en: Step by step, risked with life, gathers to finally become a brilliant lantern that lights the way for future generations.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means the firm vow and practice of leaving to seek the truth across rugged wastelands despite old age.

originalEn.quote_en: Wavering discipline originates not from external enemies but from internal complacency where principles have collapsed.
originalEn.meaning_en: It refers to the fundamentalist attitude of risking one's life to find accurate teachings in order to correct wrong practices.

originalEn.quote_en: The suffering experienced in the process of seeking enlightenment is the time spent shaping a vessel to fully contain the truth.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the spirit of truth-seeking and dedication that became stronger while overcoming countless adversities and life-or-death crises.

originalEn.event: Born in Pingyang, which is modern-day Linfen, Shanxi, China.
Departs from the Eastern Jin capital of Chang'an to travel to India in search of Buddhist scriptures.
Successfully crosses the Taklamakan Desert and the treacherous Pamir Mountains, arriving in Northwest India.
Travels extensively through northern India, studying Sanskrit and collecting scriptures, spending three years in Pataliputra.
Travels to Sri Lanka (the 'Lion Kingdom') to obtain more texts, then begins his perilous sea voyage home.
After surviving severe storms and an unplanned stop in Java, he finally arrives back in China at Laoshan, in modern Shandong.
Resides in Jiankang (modern Nanjing), where he translates the scriptures he collected and dictates his travelogue, 'A Record of Buddhist Kingdoms' (Foguo Ji).
Dies at Xin Monastery in Jingzhou at the approximate age of 85.

originalEn.quote_en: Victory is often found not in the clash of steel, but in the mind of the enemy.
originalEn.meaning_en: The outcome of war depends more on deception, psychological warfare, and exploiting an opponent's weaknesses than on simple military might.

originalEn.quote_en: My allegiance is to the Empire, an entity greater than any single emperor or courtier.
originalEn.meaning_en: It emphasizes the importance of maintaining a fundamental loyalty to the state and its ideals, transcending the whims of individuals or political situations.

originalEn.quote_en: The greatest test of a soldier is not how he fights with a full treasury, but how he triumphs with an empty one.
originalEn.meaning_en: True ability is revealed not when given abundant resources, but when one achieves goals through creativity and resourcefulness in the face of adversity and scarcity.

originalEn.event: Born in Germania, a fortified town in what is now modern Bulgaria.
Appointed as a general on the eastern frontier by Emperor Justin I.
Plays a key role in suppressing the Nika riots in Constantinople, saving Emperor Justinian's throne.
Leads the Vandalic War, conquering the Vandal Kingdom in North Africa in less than a year.
Leads the initial and most significant campaigns of the Gothic War to reconquer Italy from the Ostrogoths.
Captures the Ostrogothic capital of Ravenna and their king, Witiges.
In retirement, he is recalled to defend Constantinople from a Kutrigur Bulgar invasion, which he successfully repels.
Accused of conspiracy against Justinian, he is disgraced and briefly imprisoned before being pardoned.
Dies in Constantinople, a few months after his Emperor, Justinian.

originalEn.quote_en: A spark bloomed from one person's grievance can become a massive wildfire and burn away the old order covering the world.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means the power that brought about a massive resistance by rallying all the oppressed people, going beyond personal anger and injustice.

originalEn.quote_en: Freedom without the power to defend itself eventually meets a sad ending of relying on the arms of another strong one.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the harsh reality of history where they had to be protected by external forces even after achieving independence.

originalEn.quote_en: A leader who embraces the anger of the times must bear the responsibility of uncontrollable destruction along with glory.
originalEn.meaning_en: It refers to the heavy responsibility for violence and tragic consequences that occurred unintentionally in the process of standing against oppression.

originalEn.event: Born into a family of minor Ukrainian nobility.
Captured by the Ottomans at the Battle of Cecora and spent two years in captivity.
The "Chyhyryn affair" where Polish official Daniel Czapliński seizes his estate, leading to a personal vendetta that sparks a national rebellion.
Begins the Khmelnytsky Uprising, securing major victories at Zhovti Vody and Korsun.
Signs the Treaty of Zboriv, which recognizes the Cossack Hetmanate as an autonomous entity within the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth.
Suffers a major defeat at the Battle of Berestechko after being abandoned by his Tatar allies.
Signs the Treaty of Pereiaslav, placing the Cossack Hetmanate under the protection of the Tsar of Muscovy.
Dies in Chyhyryn, leaving behind a nascent but unstable state.

originalEn.quote_en: Do not wait for the sky to grant you power. Forge it yourself with iron and will.
originalEn.meaning_en: Destiny belongs not to those who wait, but to those who create it with their own skill and determination. One must carve their own path through action.

originalEn.quote_en: An insult to one's worth is a declaration of war. A true leader repays it with victory.
originalEn.meaning_en: Disrespect to one's dignity is intolerable, and the greatest way to overcome it and prove your worth is through achieving success.

originalEn.quote_en: An empire is not built by one man, but for all his people. My reign was brief, but the Turkic age I began is eternal.
originalEn.meaning_en: Great achievements are for the future of the community, beyond personal glory. A true legacy lies in an impact that endures beyond one's own lifetime.

originalEn.event: Emerged as chieftain of the Ashina Turks, vassals of the Rouran Khaganate.
Suppressed a Tiele revolt for the Rouran and requested a Rouran princess in marriage as a reward.
The Rouran Khagan Anagui refused and insulted Bumin, sparking the Turkic rebellion.
Allied with the Western Wei, married Princess Changle, and defeated the Rouran forces. Rouran Khagan Anagui committed suicide.
Declared himself Ilig Qaghan at the Otuken capital, founding the First Turkic Khaganate.
Died shortly after founding the Khaganate. His brother Istämi was appointed Yabgu (ruler) of the western territories.

originalEn.quote_en: Drinking is the joy of the Rus'. We cannot exist without that pleasure.
originalEn.meaning_en: Reportedly said when rejecting the Islamic envoys' proposal of temperance, this quote illustrates that the choice of religion involved cultural and practical considerations, not just theological ones.

originalEn.quote_en: It is not for us to slay them, but to bring them to repentance.
originalEn.meaning_en: After his conversion to Christianity, Vladimir was said to be reluctant to execute robbers, reflecting a shift in his rule from pagan ruthlessness to Christian mercy.

originalEn.quote_en: We knew not whether we were in Heaven or on Earth.
originalEn.meaning_en: Though not Vladimir's own words, this report from his envoys after seeing the Hagia Sophia in Constantinople was decisive. It shows the power of beauty and awe in shaping a nation's destiny.

originalEn.event: Born, the illegitimate son of Sviatoslav I of Kyiv.
Defeats and kills his half-brother Yaropolk, becoming Grand Prince of Kyiv.
Is baptized into Orthodox Christianity in Chersonesus, marries Byzantine princess Anna Porphyrogenita.
Organizes the mass baptism of the people of Kyiv in the Dnieper River, initiating the Christianization of Rus'.
Dies at Berestove, near Kyiv, after ruling for 35 years.

originalEn.quote_en: The biosphere is the envelope of life, i.e., the area of existence of living matter.
originalEn.meaning_en: This defines the biosphere not just as a location, but as the active domain created and sustained by life itself, emphasizing the interconnectedness of all living things and their environment.

originalEn.quote_en: Humanity taken as a whole is becoming a mighty geological force.
originalEn.meaning_en: This highlights his concept of the noosphere, where the collective consciousness, technology, and actions of humanity have begun to alter the planet on a geological scale, a precursor to the modern concept of the Anthropocene.

originalEn.quote_en: Scientific thought is a planetary phenomenon.
originalEn.meaning_en: Vernadsky saw the rise of scientific reason not as a local or cultural event, but as a new stage in the evolution of the planet itself, a force that transcends borders and transforms the biosphere into the noosphere.

originalEn.event: Born in Saint Petersburg, Russian Empire.
Graduates from Saint Petersburg State University.
Becomes a full member of the St. Petersburg Academy of Sciences.
Founds and becomes the first president of the Ukrainian Academy of Sciences in Kyiv.
Becomes director of the newly founded Radium Institute in Petrograd.
Publishes his seminal work, "The Biosphere," in Russian (French translation in 1929).
Publishes his final paper on the noosphere.
Dies in Moscow, Soviet Union.

originalEn.quote_en: It is better for the most unreliable of rockets to explode on the launch pad than to stand on it.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects his pragmatic, action-oriented philosophy that it is better to take risks and learn from failure than to do nothing at all.

originalEn.quote_en: Cosmonautics has an unlimited future, and its prospects are boundless, like the universe itself.
originalEn.meaning_en: Korolev firmly believed that space exploration was humanity's destiny and had long-term potential far beyond the immediate political competition.

originalEn.quote_en: The path is open to the stars.
originalEn.meaning_en: This phrase encapsulates his sense of accomplishment and optimism that humanity had overcome the technical and mental barriers to reaching space.

originalEn.event: Born in Zhytomyr, Russian Empire (now Ukraine).
Co-founds the Group for the Study of Reactive Motion (GIRD), a key Soviet rocketry research group.
Arrested during Stalin's Great Purge and sentenced to 10 years in a labor camp.
Released from prison by a special decree, though his conviction was not cleared until 1957.
As Chief Designer, he oversees the successful launch of Sputnik 1, the world's first artificial satellite.
Oversees the Vostok 1 mission, which makes Yuri Gagarin the first human to orbit the Earth.
Dies during surgery. His identity as the 'Chief Designer' is finally revealed to the public, and he is buried with state honors in the Kremlin Wall Necropolis.

originalEn.quote_en: The unknown land is not a source of fear, but of opportunity.
originalEn.meaning_en: Dezhnev viewed the harsh Siberian landscape not as a threat, but as a place to find new resources like fur, ivory, and geographical discovery. This reflects his pragmatic and courageous approach to the unknown.

originalEn.quote_en: Duty is stronger than the storm.
originalEn.meaning_en: As a Cossack, his life was defined by service to the Tsar and the completion of his mission. Despite the dangers of exploration and personal hardship, he remained committed to his duties of collecting tribute and securing territory.

originalEn.quote_en: Truth sleeps in the archives, to be awakened when its time comes.
originalEn.meaning_en: His greatest achievement was forgotten for nearly 100 years. This symbolizes that even a monumental discovery may not be immediately recognized, but that accurate records can ensure history eventually acknowledges its value.

originalEn.event: Born in Veliky Ustyug, Russia.
Recruited for Siberian service as a Cossack.
Moves to the Yakutsk ostrog (fort).
Embarks on the epic sea voyage from the Kolyma River, sailing around the Chukchi Peninsula.
After being shipwrecked, reaches the Anadyr River and establishes the Anadyrsk ostrog.
Discovers a major walrus rookery, a key source of valuable ivory.
Travels overland from Yakutsk to Moscow to deliver tribute, a journey that takes over a year.
Dies in Moscow while on official business.
German historian Gerhardt Friedrich Müller discovers Dezhnev's reports in the Yakutsk archives, finally bringing his achievement to light.

originalEn.quote_en: The prosperity of an era is remembered forever not by armed force or wealth, but through the noble art that sings of that era.
originalEn.meaning_en: It means the achievement of making the nation's pride immortal by leaving a literary work representing the country's golden age.

originalEn.quote_en: True chivalry exists not at the tip of a sword, but in a noble heart that bestows tolerance, friendship, and love.
originalEn.meaning_en: It refers to the insight that awakens us to the fact that altruistic love and loyalty are humanity's greatest virtues, rather than violence or conquest.

originalEn.quote_en: The greatest writing erases the author's name and brightly shines on the world solely with the truth it embraces.
originalEn.meaning_en: It shows the fact that he left a great mark on history solely with his work, even though his personal life is hidden behind a veil of mystery.

originalEn.event: Believed to have been born in the Rustavi region of Georgia.
Active during the reign of Queen Tamar, serving in her court, possibly as the Royal Treasurer (Mechurchletukhutsesi).
Composed his magnum opus, 'The Knight in the Panther's Skin'.
According to legend, he retired to the Georgian Monastery of the Cross in Jerusalem.
Estimated year of death, possibly in Jerusalem.

originalEn.quote_en: Even in the darkest of ages, the embers of knowledge are never extinguished. It is our duty to find them and rekindle the flame.
originalEn.meaning_en: This expresses the importance of preserving scholarship and wisdom even during times of political and religious turmoil. Leo himself continued to study ancient texts during the Iconoclast era, keeping the flame of knowledge alive.

originalEn.quote_en: Logic is a tool given by God, through which we can understand the order of the created world.
originalEn.meaning_en: Leo did not separate faith and reason. He believed that rational thought and logic were pathways to a deeper understanding of God's creation, a belief that formed the foundation of his philosophy and teaching.

originalEn.quote_en: The knowledge of one person is a treasure for the entire empire. To lose it is a greater loss than to lose a fortress.
originalEn.meaning_en: This quote reflects the episode where the Emperor persuaded Leo to stay after he was invited by the Caliph. It symbolizes the recognition of intellectual capital as a national asset and highlights Leo's own importance to the empire.

originalEn.event: Born in Thessaly, Byzantine Empire.
Moved to Constantinople to teach privately after studying on the island of Andros.
Appointed Archbishop of Thessalonica by Emperor Theophilos.
Deposed as Archbishop following the official end of Byzantine Iconoclasm.
His fame reaches the Abbasid Caliph, who invites him to Baghdad. Leo refuses, gaining favor with the Byzantine Emperor.
Appointed head (scholarch) of the newly re-founded Magnaura School by the regent Bardas.
Last known historical mention of him. His exact death date is unknown.

originalEn.quote_en: A man's worth is not in his years, but in his deeds.
originalEn.meaning_en: Emphasizes that a meaningful life is defined by one's actions and contributions, not by longevity.

originalEn.quote_en: He who has much laughter has much sorrow.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects a deep understanding of the human condition, suggesting that those who experience great joy are also capable of profound sadness, highlighting the duality of life.

originalEn.quote_en: If you wish to be rich, learn a trade. Riches diminish with time, but a skill is a source of wealth that never dries up.
originalEn.meaning_en: This promotes the value of practical skills and knowledge over material wealth, advocating for self-reliance and continuous learning as the true path to prosperity.

originalEn.event: Born as Ibrahim Qunanbaiuly in the Chingis Mountains, modern-day Kazakhstan.
Attended a madrasa in Semipalatinsk, followed by a Russian school, giving him a unique dual education.
Began to write poetry with serious dedication, adopting the pen name 'Abai'.
Wrote his major philosophical work, the 'Book of Words' (Kara Sozder).
Suffered the death of his son, Abdirakhman.
Died in the Zhidebay valley, shortly after the death of his other son, Magauiya.

originalEn.quote_en: We must not step aside from the traditions of our holy fathers, not by a hair's breadth.
originalEn.meaning_en: This encapsulates Avvakum's core belief: the absolute and unchangeable nature of religious tradition, which he felt was being corrupted by reforms.

originalEn.quote_en: I am a sinner... but I am no heretic!
originalEn.meaning_en: A powerful declaration from his autobiography, distinguishing between personal moral failings (which he readily admits) and what he saw as the ultimate sin of betraying the true faith.

originalEn.quote_en: Let them burn me. If I am burned, my soul will go to my Lord Christ, in whose name I have suffered.
originalEn.meaning_en: This shows his ultimate acceptance of martyrdom, viewing his death not as a defeat but as a final act of devotion and a path to salvation.

originalEn.event: Born in the village of Grigorovo, Nizhny Novgorod region, into a priestly family.
Begins his public opposition to the liturgical reforms initiated by Patriarch Nikon of Moscow.
Arrested and exiled to Siberia with his family. Endures a decade of extreme hardship and travel.
Briefly returns to Moscow but refuses to compromise, leading to his re-arrest and exile to the Russian North.
Defrocked and anathematized by the Great Moscow Synod. Imprisoned in an underground pit in Pustozersk.
Writes his autobiography, 'The Life of Avvakum, Written by Himself', a foundational work of Russian literature.
Burned at the stake in Pustozersk along with his closest followers for 'great blasphemies against the Tsar'.

originalEn.quote_en: There, where I have passed, the grass will never grow again.
originalEn.meaning_en: This statement symbolizes the absolute destruction and terror brought by Attila's armies, showcasing his ruthless method of conquest.

originalEn.quote_en: A united people, under a single will, is a force that can shake the foundations of the world.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects Attila's core leadership philosophy of unifying the scattered Hunnic and other tribes into a formidable empire.

originalEn.quote_en: It is a miserable thing to be afraid of death, which is an inevitable end.
originalEn.meaning_en: This quote represents the fatalistic worldview of a warrior culture, highlighting Attila's fearless nature in the face of constant warfare.

originalEn.event: Becomes co-ruler of the Huns with his brother Bleda.
Murders Bleda to become the sole ruler of the Hunnic Empire.
Launches a major invasion of the Balkans and threatens Constantinople.
Invades Gaul and fights the Battle of the Catalaunian Plains.
Invades Italy, sacks several cities, but spares Rome.
Dies on his wedding night; his empire collapses shortly after.

originalEn.quote_en: He who is without love is without faith.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes that the core of true faith lies in a deep and sincere love for God and all of creation. Love is the foundation of the spiritual journey.

originalEn.quote_en: Listen to the words of the wise and act upon them; flee from the ignorant and go far away.
originalEn.meaning_en: This teaches the importance of seeking guidance from wise teachers for spiritual growth and avoiding ignorant, worldly influences that darken the soul.

originalEn.quote_en: Those who love the world cannot say they truly love God.
originalEn.meaning_en: This indicates the incompatibility of attachment to the transient world with devotion to the eternal God. The true spiritual path requires transcending worldly desires.

originalEn.event: Born in Sayram, now in Kazakhstan.
Travels to Bukhara to study under the renowned Sufi master Yusuf Hamadani.
Briefly becomes the head of Hamadani's khaneqah (Sufi lodge) before returning to his hometown of Yasi.
At age 63, in mourning for the Prophet Muhammad, he retreats to an underground cell (chilla-khana) to live out his life in contemplation.
Dies in the city of Yasi (present-day Turkistan, Kazakhstan), where his mausoleum now stands.

originalEn.quote_en: For one purpose, and one only, do I write this, to solace my own grief.
originalEn.meaning_en: In the preface to the 'Alexiad,' she frankly states that her motivation for writing history was to overcome the personal sorrow and pain of her political defeat.

originalEn.quote_en: For history, I think, is a kind of safeguard, and a treasury of the deeds of the past.
originalEn.meaning_en: She viewed history as a vital means of preserving and evaluating the deeds of the past, intending to provide lessons for future generations.

originalEn.quote_en: A truly educated person is not the one who has mastered all sciences, but the one who has disciplined their own soul.
originalEn.meaning_en: This reflects her emphasis not just on accumulating knowledge, but on the importance of character development and self-reflection through scholarship.

originalEn.event: Born in the Porphyra chamber of the Great Palace of Constantinople, as the first child of Emperor Alexios I Komnenos and Irene Doukaina.
As a teenager, she witnesses the arrival of the leaders of the First Crusade in Constantinople, an event she would later chronicle in detail.

Her father, Alexios I, dies. She and her mother attempt to persuade him to name her husband, Nikephoros Bryennios, as his successor, but fail. She then conspires against her brother, the new Emperor John II.
After her plot is discovered, she is stripped of her property and exiled to the Kecharitomene Monastery, which her mother had founded.
Following the death of her husband, she begins writing the Alexiad, a 15-book history of her father's reign, likely completing a work her husband had started.
Dies in the monastery, having completed her monumental historical work.

originalEn.quote_en: To paint an icon is a form of prayer, and every brushstroke is a whisper to God.
originalEn.meaning_en: This emphasizes that icon-making is not merely an artistic activity, but a deeply spiritual communion with the divine, where the creative process itself is an act of devotion.

originalEn.quote_en: True beauty lies in the divine harmony that can be found even amidst the chaos of the world.
originalEn.meaning_en: His art was an attempt to transcend a painful reality and capture eternal spiritual truths of order, peace, and love, suggesting that these can be perceived even in troubled times.

originalEn.quote_en: Color is not mere pigment, but a vessel for divine light. Gold is the glory of God; blue, the mystery of Heaven.
originalEn.meaning_en: For an iconographer, color is symbolic, and each hue is part of a theological language intended to lead the viewer beyond the material world to a spiritual reality.

originalEn.event: Born, likely in the Principality of Moscow, during the era of the Tatar Yoke.
Collaborated with Theophanes the Greek and Prokhor of Gorodets on the frescoes for the Cathedral of the Annunciation in the Moscow Kremlin.
Painted frescoes and the iconostasis in the Dormition Cathedral in Vladimir, alongside his friend and collaborator Daniil Chyorny.
Painted his most famous work, the icon of 'The Trinity', for the iconostasis of the Trinity Cathedral at the Trinity Lavra of St. Sergius.
Died at the Andronikov Monastery in Moscow, where he had painted his last works in the Saviour Cathedral.
Canonized as a saint by the Russian Orthodox Church, with his feast day on July 4th.

originalEn.quote_en: God is not in power, but in truth.
originalEn.meaning_en: Expresses the belief that true strength comes not from military or political might, but from moral and spiritual truth.

originalEn.quote_en: He who comes to us with a sword, shall die by the sword.
originalEn.meaning_en: A resolute warning to any who would invade the lands of Rus', showing a determination to meet aggression with force.

originalEn.quote_en: There is no greater love than to lay down one's life for one's country.
originalEn.meaning_en: Highlights the value of ultimate sacrifice for the protection of one's land and people, signifying patriotism and duty.

originalEn.event: Born in Pereslavl-Zalessky, the second son of Prince Yaroslav Vsevolodovich.
Becomes Prince of Novgorod after his father leaves for Kiev.
Leads his army to a decisive victory over the Swedes at the Battle of the Neva, earning him the name 'Nevsky'.
Defeats the Livonian Order (a branch of the Teutonic Knights) at the Battle on the Ice on Lake Peipus.
Travels with his brother Andrey to the Mongol capital of Karakorum to be invested as princes by the Great Khan.
Travels to the Golden Horde, receives the yarlik (patent of office) and becomes the Grand Prince of Vladimir, the senior Russian prince.
Travels to the Horde's capital, Sarai, to dissuade the Khan from a planned military conscription in Russian cities, which had caused popular uprisings.
Dies in Gorodets on his way back from Sarai. He is buried in the city of Vladimir.
Canonized as a saint of the Russian Orthodox Church for his defense of the faith and the nation.

originalEn.quote_en: Deception can be raised to a virtue, and truth can be spoken in such a way that it is a lie.
originalEn.meaning_en: This means that the presentation and intent behind words matter more than their literal truth.

originalEn.quote_en: The soul of another is a dark forest.
originalEn.meaning_en: This implies that it is impossible to truly know another person's inner thoughts and motivations.

originalEn.quote_en: Habit is a second nature, and it is given to us from above.
originalEn.meaning_en: This suggests that our routines and habits shape our character and destiny, almost as if by divine decree.

originalEn.event: Born in Moscow.
Attends the Imperial Lyceum at Tsarskoye Selo.
Publishes his first long poem, 'Ruslan and Ludmila'; exiled for political verses.
Writes the historical drama 'Boris Godunov'.
Marries Natalya Goncharova; completes 'Eugene Onegin'.
Fatally wounded in a duel with Georges d'Anthès and dies two days later.

originalEn.quote_en: What is needed in the most desperate moment is not flashy pride, but the cold courage to compromise in order to survive.
